mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of Elastic Stack components on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic services in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing an operator and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Add the Elastic Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install ECK.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the ECK operator to manage Elastic Stack resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for ECK.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace.

```bash

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0

```

> Installs the ECK operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the ECK operator installation
steps.description:
Check if the ECK operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace elastic-system

```

> This command lists the pods in the elastic-system namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a Custom Resource.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

```

> This command creates a simple Elasticsearch cluster with 3 nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
Ensure that the Elasticsearch cluster is up and running.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch

```

> This command checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all managed Elasticsearch clusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the namespace where the operator was installed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Elasticsearch data. The upgrade process involves uninstalling the current version and installing the new version.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get pods --namespace elastic-system

```

> Backup the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded operator is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually occurs if the CRDs are already installed or there is a version mismatch.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and ensure they match the required versions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Elasticsearch nodes are not starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for errors and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Elasticsearch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service is not exposed correctly or network policies are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly configured and check network policies with 'kubectl get networkpolicy'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release was not installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the ECK operator with 'helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.13, ensure that all Elasticsearch clusters are compatible with version 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
